input.background: Exercise 13.1 presented data on birth weights of infants based on the smoking status of mothers. Table 13.3 lists the data. Descriptive statistics are shown in Table 13.10.
input.question: Conduct an ANOVA on these data. Show all hypothesis-testing steps. Step 3: Find the p-value.

output.answer: $P$-value. $P<0.05$ by Table D (use the 3 and $20 d f$ column). $P=0.042$ by a software utility. Either way, this provides good evidence against the null hypothesis.
